title: 'If you're so afraid of failing, you'll never experience the winning' — May 2019 risk-tolerance line
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-line Apple Note from May 20, 2019 — title "If you're so afraid of failing, you'll never experience the winning" + body "Afraid of the outcome, won't get anything great or beneficial from it." End-of-junior-year, Yahir is 16 going on 17.
  • Direct precursor to the August 2019 "fear of not doing is greater than fear of doing it" line that becomes the fear of not doing axiom — same logic, three months earlier, in slightly less compressed form.
  • Belongs to the manifesto-style title + clarifying body sub-form of the manifesto style note genre. Title carries the tweetable line; body is the subordinate-clause restatement that proves Yahir was actually working out the logic, not just reciting a stock line.
  • Sits in the same May 2019 cluster as scholarship-essay drafting and end-of-school-year wrap. Likely written in the lead-up to FBLA/Ford decisions arriving — the "afraid of the outcome" framing reads more like afraid of opening the email than abstract risk philosophy.
  • The win/outcome dichotomy here is identical to the disconnected from outcome frame Yahir uses years later — except in 2019 he's still arguing for attachment to outcomes (winning), and by 2024-2025 he's argued himself into detachment from them. Specific direction of evolution worth tracking.

Key quote

"If you're so afraid of failing, you'll never experience the winning"

"Afraid of the outcome, won't get anything great or beneficial from it"

Why it matters in the graph

  • One of the cleanest pre-articulation specimens of fear of not doing. Gives a 3-month earlier date for the engine and shows the path the framing took — it started as win/lose (May), became do/not-do (August). The August reframe is a major upgrade because it removes the outcome-attachment step.
  • Pair against 2019 10 16 life is meaningless without a pursuit (success-is-rented) and the earlier Dec 2018 obsession line — together they make the 2018-2019 risk-and-meaning trilogy that becomes the operating philosophy for the entire grants pass teen era.

Open questions

  • Was a specific failure or scholarship rejection sitting under this note?
  • Does the "win" word survive into 2020+ notes, or does the disconnected from outcome reframe replace it entirely?

Wikilinks

fear of not doing · disconnected from outcome · grants pass teen era · success is rented · legacy question · fbla
